assumptions: The intragroup dividend has already been declared and recorded as an unconditional receivable.
The receivable is denominated in a currency different from the relevant functional currency used for the consolidated foreign-exchange assessment.
The question is about hedge documentation in the consolidated financial statements, not separate statutory accounts.
No fact pattern is provided showing that the dividend receivable is itself part of a qualifying net investment in a foreign operation.

approaches.label: Foreign currency hedge of a recognized monetary dividend receivable
approaches.reasoning: An unconditional receivable is a recognized financial asset, and foreign-exchange gains or losses on monetary items are recognized in profit or loss. In a qualifying hedging relationship involving monetary items, the foreign-currency component is also presented in profit or loss.
conditions: the dividend is already an unconditional, recognized receivable
the receivable is a foreign-currency monetary item in the consolidated analysis
a qualifying hedge relationship is formally designated and documented
approaches.practical_implication: The hedge would be documented to offset the receivable's FX remeasurement in profit or loss rather than through OCI.

source_contributions.summary: B3.1.2 supports recognition of an unconditional receivable as a financial asset.
B5.7.2 requires FX gains and losses on foreign-currency monetary financial assets/liabilities to be recognized in profit or loss.
B5.7.4 indicates that in a hedging relationship between non-derivative monetary items, the foreign-currency component is presented in profit or loss.

approaches.label: Net investment hedge in a foreign operation
approaches.reasoning: IFRIC 16 limits this model to foreign-currency risk on a net investment in a foreign operation, where the hedged item is an amount of net assets. A dividend receivable, as stated, is not presented in the context as a net-investment exposure.
approaches.practical_implication: The OCI net-investment hedge model should not be used for the dividend receivable on the facts given.

source_contributions.summary: Paras 10-14 restrict net investment hedge accounting to foreign-currency risk on net investments in foreign operations and define the qualifying hedged item as net assets.
Paras 13-17 add that the same risk can qualify only once in consolidation, the hedging instrument may be held anywhere in the group, and effective amounts go to OCI until disposal.
B5.7.2 treats net investment hedges as an exception to the normal profit-or-loss treatment of FX on monetary items.

approaches.label: No hedge accounting; ordinary foreign exchange treatment
approaches.reasoning: If no qualifying hedge documentation is applied, foreign-exchange gains and losses on a foreign-currency monetary receivable remain recognized in profit or loss under the normal rule.
approaches.practical_implication: The FX effect of the dividend receivable is left in profit or loss with no hedge reserve or special hedge presentation.

source_contributions.summary: B5.7.2 requires FX gains and losses on foreign-currency monetary financial assets/liabilities to be recognized in profit or loss unless a specified hedge exception applies.
5.7.2 and 5.7.3 distinguish ordinary profit-or-loss treatment from hedge-accounting treatment.
